testScenario: Verify that a registered user can log in with valid credentials so that they are redirected to the homepage.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User has a registered Amazon account.
3. Test Data: Email 'testuser@gmail.com', Password 'Test@1234'.
testSteps:
1. Navigate to https://www.amazon.com.
2. Click the 'Sign In' button on the top navigation bar.
3. Enter 'testuser@gmail.com' into the 'Email' field.
4. Click the 'Continue' button.
5. Enter 'Test@1234' into the 'Password' field.
6. Click the 'Sign In' submit button.
expectedResult: User is successfully authenticated and redirected to the Amazon homepage with their account name visible in the navigation bar.

testScenario: Verify that a user cannot log in with an invalid password so that an appropriate error message is shown.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User has a registered Amazon account.
3. Test Data: Email 'testuser@gmail.com', Password 'WrongPass999'.
testSteps:
1. Navigate to https://www.amazon.com.
2. Click the 'Sign In' button.
3. Enter 'testuser@gmail.com' into the 'Email' field.
4. Click the 'Continue' button.
5. Enter 'WrongPass999' into the 'Password' field.
6. Click the 'Sign In' submit button.
expectedResult: An error message 'Your password is incorrect' is displayed and the user remains on the login page.

testScenario: Verify that submitting the login form with an empty email field so that a validation error is displayed.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is on the Amazon Sign In page.
testSteps:
1. Navigate to https://www.amazon.com/ap/signin.
2. Leave the 'Email' field blank.
3. Click the 'Continue' button.
expectedResult: A validation error 'Enter your email or mobile phone number' is displayed below the email field.
testdata: {"Validation.field": "email", "Validation.message": "Enter your email or mobile phone number", "Validation.status": "FAILED"}

testScenario: Verify that searching for a valid product returns relevant results so that the user can browse and select items.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in to Amazon.
3. Test Data: Search query 'wireless headphones'.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon.
2. Locate the search bar at the top of the homepage.
3. Enter 'wireless headphones' into the search bar.
4. Click the search icon or press Enter.
5. Observe the search results page.
expectedResult: The search results page displays a list of products related to 'wireless headphones' with product names, prices, and ratings visible.

testScenario: Verify that searching with an empty query so that the search bar shows a validation prompt or returns homepage.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in to Amazon.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon.
2. Click on the search bar without entering any text.
3. Click the search icon or press Enter.
expectedResult: Either a validation message 'Please enter a search term' is shown or the page reloads with no results and a prompt to enter a keyword.

testScenario: Verify that a user can add a product to the cart so that it appears in the cart with correct details.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in to Amazon.
3. Test Data: Product 'Sony WH-1000XM5 Headphones'.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon.
2. Search for 'Sony WH-1000XM5 Headphones'.
3. Click on the first product result.
4. On the product detail page, click the 'Add to Cart' button.
5. Navigate to the cart by clicking the cart icon.
expectedResult: The product appears in the cart with the correct name, quantity of 1, and price displayed.

testScenario: Verify that a user can update the quantity of a cart item so that the total price updates accordingly.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in and has at least one item in the cart.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon and navigate to the cart.
2. Locate a product in the cart.
3. Change the quantity dropdown from 1 to 2.
4. Observe the updated subtotal.
expectedResult: The cart subtotal updates to reflect the new quantity and the correct price is displayed.

testScenario: Verify that a user can remove a product from the cart so that the cart is updated and shows empty or remaining items.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in and has one item in the cart.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon and navigate to the cart.
2. Locate the product in the cart.
3. Click the 'Delete' link below the product name.
4. Observe the cart state.
expectedResult: The product is removed from the cart. If no other items exist, the cart shows 'Your Amazon Cart is empty'.

testScenario: Verify that a logged-in user can add a product to their wishlist so that it appears under 'Saved for Later' or their list.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in to Amazon.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon.
2. Search for any product and open the product detail page.
3. Click the 'Add to List' button (heart icon or dropdown).
4. Select 'Create a new list' or an existing wishlist.
5. Navigate to the wishlist page.
expectedResult: The product appears on the wishlist page with correct name and price.

testScenario: Verify that applying a price range filter on search results so that only products within the range are displayed.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User has performed a search for 'laptops'.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon and search for 'laptops'.
2. On the search results page, locate the 'Price' filter on the left panel.
3. Enter min price '500' and max price '1000'.
4. Click 'Go' to apply the filter.
expectedResult: All displayed products fall within the $500–$1000 price range. Products outside this range are not shown.

testScenario: Verify filtering products by minimum 4-star rating so that only highly rated products are displayed.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User has searched for 'bluetooth speakers'.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon and search for 'bluetooth speakers'.
2. On the search results page, click '4 Stars & Up' in the 'Avg. Customer Review' section.
3. Observe the filtered results.
expectedResult: Only products with an average rating of 4 stars or above are displayed in the results.

testScenario: Verify that a user can proceed to checkout and view the order summary so that details are correct before placing the order.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in and has one item in the cart with a saved delivery address.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon and add a product to the cart.
2. Click the 'Proceed to Buy' button in the cart.
3. Confirm or enter a delivery address.
4. Review the order summary page.
expectedResult: The order summary page displays the correct product, quantity, price, delivery address, and estimated delivery date.

testScenario: Verify that a user can add a new delivery address in their account settings so that it is saved and available at checkout.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in to Amazon.
3. Test Data: Name 'John Doe', Address '123 Main St', City 'Seattle', State 'WA', ZIP '98101'.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon.
2. Navigate to 'Account & Lists' > 'Your Account'.
3. Click on 'Manage address book'.
4. Click 'Add address'.
5. Fill in Name 'John Doe', Address '123 Main St', City 'Seattle', State 'WA', ZIP '98101'.
6. Click 'Add address' to save.
expectedResult: The new address is saved and appears in the address book and is selectable during checkout.

testScenario: Verify that a user can view their order history so that past orders are listed with correct details.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is logged in and has placed at least one previous order.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon.
2. Navigate to 'Returns & Orders' at the top right.
3. Observe the list of past orders.
4. Click on any order to view its details.
expectedResult: The orders page displays all past orders with order ID, date, total, and status. Clicking an order shows full details.

testScenario: Verify that a logged-in user can sign out successfully so that the session is terminated and they are redirected to the homepage.
prerequisites:
1. System: Chrome v122, Windows 11.
2. User is currently logged in to Amazon.
testSteps:
1. Log in to Amazon.
2. Hover over 'Account & Lists' in the top navigation bar.
3. Click 'Sign Out' from the dropdown menu.
4. Observe the page after sign out.
expectedResult: The user is signed out and redirected to the Amazon homepage. The navigation bar shows 'Hello, Sign in' instead of the user's name.
